era_context: Neo-Assyrian Empire under Shalmaneser III. Levantine kingdoms remain under Assyrian hegemony. Egypt is fragmented under the late 21st Dynasty. Greek city-states continue their development. Phoenician maritime expansion continues. Chinese chronology for this period is uncertain.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III continued his annual military campaigns into the Levant as part of his systematic strategy to maintain Assyrian hegemony and extract tribute from vassal states. These campaigns are documented in Assyrian royal inscriptions and represent the continuation of a well-established pattern of imperial expansion.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign and annual campaigns are well-documented. The specific year 806 BCE is assigned by chronological convention.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for 806 BCE. The year is historiographically assigned to Shalmaneser III's reign but has no documented distinctiveness.
historiographic_note: 806 BCE is poorly documented. The year is included for chronological continuity. No specific events are known with certainty. The historical record remains dominated by Assyrian sources.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's campaign sequence
